name: Fig Newtons
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, cream butter and sugar until very light and thick.
2. Add eggs one at a time; beat in vanilla extract.
3. Sift together flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt. Mix well with the creamed mixture.
4. Turn out onto a sheet of floured plastic wrap; refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
5. Cut stems off figs. Combine figs and water in a saucepan; simmer covered until fruit is soft (30 minutes).
6. Add sugar, lemon rind, and salt; continue simmering covered (15 minutes longer). Press mixture through a food mill to cool.
7. Preheat oven to 350°F. Prepare baking sheet with parchment or spray for release.
8. Divide chilled dough into thirds. Refrigerate two parts.
9. Roll remaining piece on well-floured surface to form a rectangle about 5 x 11 inches.
10. Spread one-third of fig filling slightly along the length, leaving a 1/2-inch margin around edges. Mound filling in center along whole length. Moisten edges with water.
11. Gently lift uncovered pastry and fold over filling. Press halves together to seal. Trim edges; shape roll into a neat half-cylinder about 11 inches long and 2-2.5 inches wide.
12. Place on prepared baking sheet. Repeat until all filling and dough are used.
13. Bake for 25 minutes, or until slightly browned. Cool rolls on rack before trimming with a sharp serrated knife to slices about 1.5 inches wide. Store in an airtight container when cooled.
